title: Credential Compromise: Stolen Card Used Across Multiple Shipping Addresses in Rolling Week
narrative: Fraudster has obtained valid card credentials and is using them to ship goods to multiple drop addresses within a short time window. Each individual transaction appears legitimate with correct billing information and reasonable amounts, but card-level aggregation reveals the card is associated with an unusually high count of distinct shipping addresses over the past seven days. This pattern exploits the fact that legitimate cardholders typically ship to one or two addresses (home, work) while fraudsters cycle through multiple drop locations to receive goods before the cardholder notices. The velocity of address diversity, not transaction count or amount, is the primary signal. Device fingerprints may show consistency as the fraudster uses the same compromised session or device to place multiple orders.
indicators: C8 > 24 indicating card used with more than p90 threshold of distinct addresses in recent window
C13 between 7 and 24 showing elevated but not extreme transaction count in 7-day period
card1_distinct_addr1 >= 6 reflecting multiple unique shipping addresses linked to same card
TransactionAmt between 60 and 200 keeping individual purchase amounts within typical cardholder range
D1 <= 3 suggesting device-card pairing is recent but not first-time
C14 between 2 and 7 indicating moderate product category diversity without extreme outliers
indicator_explanations: Card has been used with far more different addresses than a typical legitimate cardholder uses
Transaction frequency is elevated but not high enough to trigger velocity-based fraud rules
Same card number appears with six or more unique shipping destinations in recent history
Each purchase amount looks normal for the cardholder and does not raise amount-based flags
Device has been paired with this card recently suggesting ongoing fraudulent session activity
Purchases span several product categories consistent with fraudster testing card across merchant types
summary: Stolen card credentials are being exploited to ship merchandise to multiple drop addresses within a week. Each transaction appears normal in isolation, but card-level address diversity is far above legitimate cardholder patterns.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders rarely ship to more than two or three addresses in a short period, while fraudsters using stolen credentials cycle through multiple drop locations to maximize goods received before detection. The elevated address-count aggregation (C8) combined with moderate transaction counts reveals systematic credential abuse.
